title: Unity Shader Material Properties — Built-in / URP / HDRP
summary: Canonical property name mapping for Unity's main Lit and Unlit shaders across Built-in / URP / HDRP render pipelines. Use when setting material properties programmatically via Material.SetColor / SetFloat / SetTexture, or mosaic tools material/set-property and material/create. Property internal names differ between pipelines; using the wrong name either silently writes to a hidden backward-compat alias (URP Lit) or throws 'Material doesn't have a property'. This entry provides the exact property name per pipeline plus decision rules for cross-pipeline code.

llmGuidance.when_asked_to_change_material_color: Detect active pipeline via GraphicsSettings.currentRenderPipeline. URP → _BaseColor. Built-in → _Color. HDRP → _BaseColor. If pipeline unknown, prefer _BaseColor and fall back to _Color with HasProperty guard.
llmGuidance.when_creating_new_colored_material: Use material/create with shaderName from full.shaders. For URP: 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit'. For Built-in: 'Standard'. For HDRP: 'HDRP/Lit'. Then set the base-color property. Default _BaseMap is white, so no texture is required for a flat color.
llmGuidance.when_setting_emission: Built-in and URP use _EmissionColor + _EmissionMap. HDRP uses _EmissiveColor + _EmissiveColorMap. Branch by pipeline. Enable keyword _EMISSION on Built-in and URP materials.
llmGuidance.when_setting_normal_map: Built-in and URP Lit use _BumpMap. HDRP uses _NormalMap. Normal scale is _BumpScale (Built-in/URP) or _NormalScale (HDRP).
llmGuidance.when_debugging_'material_looks_unchanged: First check property name vs pipeline. URP Lit ships hidden _Color and _MainTex aliases for backward compat — SetColor('_Color', ...) on URP returns no error but writes to the unused alias slot. Use _BaseColor and guard with Material.HasProperty in cross-pipeline code.
llmGuidance.when_unsure_which_pipeline_the_project_u: Read GraphicsSettings.currentRenderPipeline. Null = Built-in. Non-null asset typename contains 'UniversalRenderPipelineAsset' (URP) or 'HDRenderPipelineAsset' (HDRP). See kb-rendering-pipeline-detection for API details.

built-in-standard.use: Legacy Built-in Render Pipeline PBR workflow.

urp-lit.use: URP main physically-based shader. Default for most URP projects.

_Color.writeEffect: writes to alias slot only; not rendered

_MainTex.writeEffect: writes to alias slot only; not rendered

urp-simple-lit.use: Blinn-Phong lighting. Cheaper than Lit. Mobile / stylized.

urp-unlit.use: URP unlit. Effects, UI, custom rendering.

hdrp-lit.use: HDRP main PBR shader with subsurface / clearcoat / anisotropy.
notes: HDRP Lit uses _BaseColorMap (not _BaseMap) and _EmissiveColor (not _EmissionColor). Distinct from URP.
Additional properties for clearcoat / anisotropy / subsurface exist — consult shader source when those features are needed.

decisionRules.scenario: Creating a visible colored material via mosaic/material/create + set-property
decisionRules.rule: 1) Detect pipeline (see kb-rendering-pipeline-detection). 2) Pick shader name from full.shaders.<pipeline>.shaderName. 3) Set base-color property per full.pipelineComparisonQuickTable. Default _BaseMap is white, so a single color call is enough.
decisionRules.evidence: Gemini session 2026-04-20 spent 3-4 iterations confusing _Color vs _BaseColor when creating red cube and white plane under URP.

decisionRules.scenario: Setting a property on a material whose shader you don't fully know
decisionRules.rule: Guard Set* calls with Material.HasProperty(name). Do not rely on silent success — URP Lit accepts _Color without error but writes to a hidden alias, producing a silent visual bug.
decisionRules.evidence: Unity Discussions thread on missing _Color property. URP Lit shader source declares [HideInInspector] _Color and _MainTex as obsolete aliases.

decisionRules.scenario: Legacy code calls SetColor('_Color', ...) on a URP material and the color doesn't change
decisionRules.rule: This is the backward-compat alias trap. Replace with SetColor('_BaseColor', ...). Treat all _Color / _MainTex uses in URP contexts as suspect and fix them in bulk.
decisionRules.evidence: URP Lit.shader declares _Color and _MainTex as [HideInInspector] backward-compat aliases — they accept writes but are unused in rendering.

decisionRules.scenario: Cross-pipeline code needs to set emission
decisionRules.rule: Branch on pipeline: Built-in/URP → _EmissionColor + _EmissionMap + EnableKeyword('_EMISSION'). HDRP → _EmissiveColor + _EmissiveColorMap (HDRP keyword handling differs — check full.shaders.hdrp-lit.notes).

commonPitfalls.symptom: Material.SetColor('_Color', ...) on URP material succeeds silently but visual doesn't change.
commonPitfalls.cause: Writing to URP Lit's hidden _Color backward-compat alias, not _BaseColor.
commonPitfalls.fix: Use _BaseColor. Verify with HasProperty before write in cross-pipeline code.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Log: "Material doesn't have a color property '_Color'".
commonPitfalls.cause: Target shader (custom URP, HDRP, or stripped variant) doesn't declare _Color.
commonPitfalls.fix: Detect pipeline, use correct property name, guard with HasProperty.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Emission works on Built-in/URP but HDRP material stays dark.
commonPitfalls.fix: Branch property name by pipeline. See full.pipelineComparisonQuickTable.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Normal map appears on Built-in/URP but not HDRP after scripted setup.
commonPitfalls.cause: HDRP uses _NormalMap; Built-in / URP Lit use _BumpMap.

pipelineDetection.summary: This entry's sibling KB (kb-rendering-pipeline-detection) covers detection in depth. Quick answer: GraphicsSettings.currentRenderPipeline returns null on Built-in, a RenderPipelineAsset on URP/HDRP. Inspect the asset typename to distinguish URP from HDRP.

versionDeltas.change: LightweightRenderPipeline (LWRP) renamed to UniversalRenderPipeline (URP). Shader names changed from 'Lightweight Render Pipeline/Lit' to 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit'. LWRP shader type names deprecated.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: Old LWRP shader references must be updated to 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit'. Affects projects migrating from pre-2019.3.

versionDeltas.change: URP Lit shader added hidden [HideInInspector] _Color and _MainTex backward-compat aliases for code written against Standard shader.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: SetColor('_Color', ...) succeeds silently on URP but writes to unused slot. Use _BaseColor. This is the source of the 'material looks unchanged' bug in the Gemini fluid session.

versionDeltas.change: Unity 6 ships URP 17.x. No property name changes for Lit shader. Additional shader features added but don't affect core property mapping.

externalReferences.title: Unity Manual — Change or detect the active pipeline

sources.title: Unity Discussions — missing _Color property on URP

Unity Manual and ScriptReference pages are cited as externalReferences and NOT reproduced here.
